name: Pooja R. Sonar
tagline: Full-stack developer specializing in scalable web applications and innovative technology solutions
about: Accomplished Software Developer with proven expertise in building scalable web applications using cutting-edge technologies. Currently pursuing B.Tech in IT with an outstanding CGPA of 9.2. Demonstrated proficiency in full-stack development with specialized expertise in MERN stack, Next.js, and TypeScript. Successfully delivered diverse projects spanning e-commerce platforms to AI-powered coding preparation systems, consistently driving innovation and technical excellence.
highlights: Developed scalable web applications using Next.js and TypeScript
Built e-commerce platform features for EV bikes marketplace
Created reusable UI components using Storybook
Won 2nd place in all Goa science day innovative project building competition
Secured third position at GDSC ADGITM hackathon

projects.name: AccessAI - CodeQuest
projects.description: Comprehensive AI-powered coding preparation platform featuring gamified learning experiences. Enables users to master programming languages, including Solidity for smart contract development, through interactive challenges and personalized learning paths.

projects.name: Maxiple Technology Website
projects.description: Professional corporate website developed for a laptop repair services company. Delivered as a complete freelance solution featuring modern design, service showcases, and client engagement capabilities.

projects.name: Chess.com Backend Clone
projects.description: Enterprise-level backend system replicating Chess.com's core functionality. Features sophisticated API architecture and database management systems supporting real-time chess gameplay, user authentication, and interactive gaming experiences.

projects.name: Arduino Floor Cleaner
projects.description: Award-winning IoT-enabled autonomous floor cleaning device with smartphone integration. Features remote control functionality through custom mobile application and advanced Arduino-based automation. Recognized with 2nd place at All Goa Science Day Competition.
